plainEnglish: Components with the same function across pages must be identified consistently—same label, same icon, same accessible name. If a search button says 'Search' on one page and 'Find' on another, users (especially screen reader users) can't tell they do the same thing.
whyItMatters: Screen reader users identify controls by their name; labeling the same function differently across pages (e.g., 'Search' vs. 'Find') makes them seem like different features.
quickCheck: Confirm controls with the same function use the same label, icon, and accessible name everywhere they appear.
commonMistakes: Labeling the same action differently across pages ('Search' vs. 'Find', 'Submit' vs. 'Send').
Using different icons for the same function in different places.
Inconsistent accessible names (aria-label) for identical controls.

codeExamples.label: Same label for the same action everywhere
codeExamples.code:
{/* This action is labeled identically on every page */}
<button>Download PDF</button>

codeExamples.label: Same action labeled differently per page
codeExamples.code:
<button>Download PDF</button>   {/* page A */}
<button>Get the file</button>  {/* page B */}
